title: चण्डी स्तोत्रम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री चण्डी स्तोत्रम् ॥

ॐ अस्य श्रीचण्डीस्तोत्रस्य मार्कण्डेय ऋषिः।
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीचण्डिका देवता।
श्रीचण्डिकाप्रीत्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः॥

ॐ नमश्चण्डिकायै॥

चण्डे चण्डमदोद्धतदैत्यदर्पनिवारिणि।
रूपं देहि जयं देहि यशो देहि द्विषो जहि॥१॥

चण्डिके सततं युद्धे जयदा कालदारिणी।
जयं देहि विभूतिं च देवि दुर्गे नमोऽस्तु ते॥२॥

रूपं विद्यां च लक्ष्मीं च सर्वसौभाग्यदायिनि।
स्वाहा स्वधा नमस्तुभ्यं नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥३॥

दुर्गे दुर्गार्तिशमनी दुर्गापद्विनिवारिणी।
दुर्गमच्छेदिनी दुर्गसाधिनी दुर्गनाशिनी॥४॥

दुर्गतोद्धारिणी दुर्गनिहन्त्री दुर्गमापहा।
दुर्गमज्ञानदा दुर्गदैत्यलोकदवानला॥५॥

दुर्गमा दुर्गमालोका दुर्गमात्मस्वरूपिणी।
दुर्गमार्गप्रदा दुर्गमविद्या दुर्गमाश्रिता॥६॥

दुर्गमज्ञानसंस्थाना दुर्गमध्यानभासिनी।
दुर्गमोहा दुर्गमगा दुर्गमार्थस्वरूपिणी॥७॥

सर्वविघ्नविनाशिन्यै सर्वदैत्यविनाशिनी।
सर्वशत्रुविनाशिन्यै देव्यै चण्डिकयै नमः॥८॥

सर्वव्याधिविनाशिन्यै सर्वमृत्युविनाशिनी।
सर्वभयक्षयकर्यै देव्यै चण्डिकयै नमः॥९॥

त्वं श्रीस्त्वमीश्वरी त्वं ह्रीस्त्वं बुद्धिर्बोधलक्षणा।
त्वं पुष्टिस्त्वं च तुष्टिस्त्वं शान्तिः क्षान्तिरेव च॥१०॥

खड्गिनी शूलिनी घोरा गदिनी चक्रिणी तथा।
शंखिनी चापिनी बाणभुशुण्डीपरिघायुधा॥११॥

सौम्या सौम्यतराशेषसौम्येभ्यस्त्वतिसुन्दरी।
परापराणां परमा त्वमेव परमेश्वरी॥१२॥

यच्च किञ्चित्क्वचिद्वस्तु सदसद्वाऽखिलात्मिके।
तस्य सर्वस्य या शक्तिः सा त्वं किं स्तूयसे तदा॥१३॥

सर्वभूता यदा देवी भुक्तिमुक्तिप्रदायिनी।
त्वां स्तुतः कः स्तवे शक्तः परमात्मस्वरूपिणीम्॥१४॥

सर्वस्य बुद्धिरूपेण जनस्य हृदि संस्थिते।
स्वर्गापवर्गदे देवि नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१५॥

कलाकाष्ठादिरूपेण परिणामप्रदायिनि।
विश्वस्योपरतौ शक्ते नारायणि नमोऽस्तु ते॥१६॥

शुभं कुरु जगद्धात्रि शुभं कुरु सुरेश्वरि।
शुभं कुरु विशालाक्षि देवि विश्वेश्वरि प्रभे॥१७॥

॥ इति श्री चण्डी स्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Chandi Stotram ||

Om Asya Shri Chandi-stotrasya Markandeya Rishiph.
Anushtup Chhandah. Shri Chandika Devata.
Shri Chandika-prityarthe Jape Viniyogah.

Om Namashchandikayai.

Chande Chanda-madoddhatadaitya-darpa-nivarini.
Rupam Dehi Jayam Dehi Yasho Dehi Dvisho Jahi.||1||

Chandike Satatam Yuddhe Jayada Kala-darini.
Jayam Dehi Vibhutim Cha Devi Durge Namo'stu Te.||2||

Rupam Vidyam Cha Lakshmim Cha Sarva-saubhagya-dayini.
Svaha Svadha Namas-tubhyam Narayani Namo'stu Te.||3||

Durge Durgarti-shamani Durgapad-vinivarini.
Durgam-achchhedini Durga-sadhini Durga-nashini.||4||

Durgato-ddharini Durga-nihantri Durgam-apaha.
Durgam-ajnana-da Durga-daitya-loka-davanala.||5||

Durgama Durgam-aloka Durgam-atma-svarupini.
Durgam-arga-prada Durgam-avidya Durgam-ashrita.||6||

Durgam-ajnana-samsthana Durgam-adhyana-bhasini.
Durgam-oha Durgam-aga Durgam-artha-svarupini.||7||

Sarva-vighna-vinashinyai Sarva-daitya-vinashini.
Sarva-shatru-vinashinyai Devyai Chandikayai Namah.||8||

Sarva-vyadhi-vinashinyai Sarva-mrityu-vinashini.
Sarva-bhaya-kshaya-karyai Devyai Chandikayai Namah.||9||

Tvam Shris Tvam Ishvari Tvam Hris Tvam Buddhir Bodha-lakshana.
Tvam Pushtis Tvam Cha Tushtis Tvam Shantih Kshantir Eva Cha.||10||

Khadgini Shulini Ghora Gadini Chakrini Tatha.
Shankhini Chapini Bana-bhushundi-parighayudha.||11||

Saumya Saumyatara-shesha-saumyebhyas Tvati-sundari.
Parapara-anam Parama Tvam Eva Parameshvari.||12||

Yach Cha Kinchit Kvachid Vastu Sadasad Va'khilatmike.
Tasya Sarvasya Ya Shaktih Sa Tvam Kim Stuyase Tada.||13||

Sarva-bhuta Yada Devi Bhukti-mukti-pradayini.
Tvam Stutah Kah Stave Shaktah Paramatma-svarupinim.||14||

Sarvasya Buddhi-rupena Janasya Hridi Samsthite.
Svargapavarga-de Devi Narayani Namo'stu Te.||15||

Kala-kashthadi-rupena Parinamapradayini.
Vishvasyoparatau Shakte Narayani Namo'stu Te.||16||

Shubham Kuru Jagaddhatri Shubham Kuru Sureshvari.
Shubham Kuru Vishalakshi Devi Vishveshvari Prabhe.||17||

|| Iti Shri Chandi Stotram Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
हे चण्डी! उन्मत्त दैत्यों के दर्प को निवारण करने वाली! रूप दो, जय दो, यश दो, शत्रुओं को नष्ट करो।

हे चण्डिके! युद्ध में सदा जय देने वाली, काल को दारण करने वाली! जय और विभूति दो। हे दुर्गा देवी! नमस्कार।

रूप, विद्या, लक्ष्मी और सर्वसौभाग्य देने वाली! स्वाहा, स्वधा, आपको नमस्कार। हे नारायणी नमस्कार।

हे दुर्गे! दुर्गति को शमन करने वाली, दुर्ग-आपदों को दूर करने वाली, दुर्गम को छेदने वाली, दुर्ग की साधिनी, दुर्ग-नाशिनी, दुर्गति से उद्धार करने वाली।

तुम श्री हो, ईश्वरी हो, ह्री हो, बुद्धि हो, पुष्टि-तुष्टि-शान्ति-क्षान्ति हो। खड्ग, शूल, गदा, चक्र, शंख, धनुष धारिणी हो।

सौम्य से भी अधिक सुन्दर, परम परमेश्वरी! जो कुछ भी कहीं सत् या असत् है, उस सबकी शक्ति तुम हो। हे जगद्धात्री! शुभ करो, हे विश्वेश्वरी! मंगल करो।

viniyog.shloka: ॐ अस्य श्रीचण्डीस्तोत्रमन्त्रस्य मार्कण्डेय ऋषिः। अनुष्टुप् छन्दः। श्रीचण्डिका देवता। ॐ बीजम्। चण्डिका शक्तिः। श्रीचण्डिकाप्रीत्यर्थे चण्डीस्तोत्रपाठे विनियोगः॥

benefits: Invokes beauty, victory, fame, and destruction of enemies
Invokes Chandika as the remover of all forms of 'durga' (difficulty)
Invokes the blessing of prosperity, good fortune, and spiritual wealth
Classical tradition invokes this for removal of diseases, fears, and the threat of untimely death
Essential hymn recited alongside the Durga Saptashati
Invokes the supreme Parameshvari for auspiciousness in all undertakings
description: Dedicated to Goddess Durga, the Chandi Stotram (चण्डी स्तोत्रम्) is a devotional hymn of praise revered in Hindu tradition. This revered text originates from Markandeya Purana (Chandi Path). Chanting this stotra with devotion is said to invoke the supreme Parameshvari for auspiciousness in all undertakings as well as remove all diseases, fears, and the threat of untimely death. Additionally, it is known to bestow prosperity, good fortune, and spiritual wealth. This sacred text is ideally recited on Tuesday (Mangalvar) and Friday (Shukravar) and during Navratri for enhanced blessings. At 17 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 10 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Chandi Stotram in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Chandi Stotram in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Powerful hymn to Goddess Chandika for victory, beauty, fame, and removal of all difficulties.
relatedSearches: Chandi Path Navratri nine days recitation

seoFaqs.question: Why is Devi Mahatmya called by three names — Chandi Stotram, Durga Saptashati, and Devi Mahatmya?
seoFaqs.answer: Drikpanchang clarifies: 'Durga Saptashati is also known as Devi Mahatmya and Chandi Patha.' The three names emphasise different aspects of the same 700-verse text: Chandi Stotram emphasises the fierce form of the Goddess (Chandi = the wrathful one); Durga Saptashati emphasises the structure (sapta-shati = seven hundred verses); Devi Mahatmya emphasises the literary genre (mahatmya = glorification text). Bengali and Eastern traditions favour 'Chandi Path,' Maharashtrian and Hindi-belt traditions prefer 'Durga Saptashati,' while South Indian Sanskritists more often use 'Devi Mahatmyam.' All refer to the identical text. (Source: Drikpanchang Durga Saptashati; Stotram.co.in Devi Mahatmyam.)

seoFaqs.question: How is the Chandi Stotram structured into chapters and stories?
seoFaqs.answer: Wikipedia confirms that 'the seven hundred verses are arranged into 13 chapters' (adhyayas), traditionally grouped into three episodes (charitas): the Prathama Charita (chapter 1, killing of Madhu and Kaitabha by Vishnu through the Goddess's intervention), the Madhyama Charita (chapters 2–4, killing of Mahishasura by Mahalakshmi), and the Uttama Charita (chapters 5–13, killing of Shumbha and Nishumbha by Mahasaraswati through Kaushiki and Kalika). The three episodes are read on three consecutive days during a triple-day Saptashati anushthana, or all together over a single day for the full Chandi Path. (Source: Wikipedia Devi Mahatmya; Stotra Nidhi Durga Saptashati.)

seoFaqs.question: Who is the human narrator and what is the frame story?
seoFaqs.answer: The Chandi Stotram is narrated by Sage Markandeya (hence its inclusion in the Markandeya Purana) and tells the story of King Suratha, who has lost his kingdom to enemies, and a merchant Samadhi, who has been thrown out of his home by his own family. Both meet in a forest hermitage and are told the three Devi-charita stories by Sage Sumedhas. The frame teaches that the Goddess restores both worldly fortune (to Suratha, who returns and reclaims his kingdom) and spiritual liberation (to Samadhi, who attains moksha) — meeting devotees at whichever level they sincerely seek. (Source: Wikipedia Devi Mahatmya; Sri RajaRajeshwari Peetham Devi Mahatmyam.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the Chandi Homam and how does it use the stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Chandi Homam is a major Vedic fire ceremony in which the entire Saptashati is recited while specific oblations of ghee, sesame, and herbal substances are offered into a sacred fire after each verse or each chapter. A simple Chandi Homam takes 5–6 hours; a Sahasra Chandi (1000-fold recitation) takes weeks and requires a team of 1000 priests; the supreme Ayuta Chandi (10,000 recitations) and Lakshachandi (100,000 recitations) are commissioned by temples and royal families for mass welfare. The Chandi Stotram is the only stotra that has scaled into such enormous Vedic-yajna formats outside of Soma sacrifices. (Source: Sage of Kanchi Devi Mahatmyam reading procedure; Sri RajaRajeshwari Peetham.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the Rahasya-traya (three secrets) appended to the Chandi Stotram?
seoFaqs.answer: Beyond the 13 chapters, traditional manuscripts append three esoteric texts known as the Rahasya-traya: the Pradhanika Rahasya (the secret of the supreme cause), the Vaikrutika Rahasya (the secret of the Goddess's emanations), and the Murti Rahasya (the secret of her iconographic forms). These three short texts are not mere appendices but are considered the philosophical key that unlocks the symbolism of the demon-killing narratives. They identify Mahalakshmi, Mahakali, and Mahasaraswati as the three primary Devi-forms and trace their relationship to Prakriti and Purusha. Stotra Nidhi includes them in any complete Saptashati publication. (Source: Stotra Nidhi Durga Saptashati Anubandham; Sri RajaRajeshwari Peetham.)

seoFaqs.question: What did the Devi Mahatmya contribute to the development of Hindu Goddess theology?
seoFaqs.answer: The Wikipedia entry on Devi Mahatmya credits it as 'the earliest extant text in which the goddess is conceived as the supreme divine ultimate reality and creator of the universe.' Composed between 400–600 CE, the Chandi Stotram is therefore the foundational scripture of Shaktism — the school that holds the Devi as Brahman, with Brahma, Vishnu, and Shiva functioning as her instruments. Before this text, goddesses appeared as consorts of male deities; after it, the Devi became theologically supreme. Every later Shakta text — Devi Bhagavata Purana, Lalita Sahasranama, Saundarya Lahari, Tripura Rahasya — builds on the Chandi's framework. (Source: Wikipedia Devi Mahatmya theological significance; Stotram.co.in Devi Mahatmyam.)
